**Direction:** C->S  
**Identifier:** `0x03/0x08`  
**Status:** verified

## Structure

C→S reliable sub-tag 0x08 — explicit reliable-ACK channel.
Verified 2026-05-10 against all 54 retail samples (11/17
captures emit it). Inner body is **always (seq-1) LE16** in the
2-byte form — the client's record of the last server-reliable
sub-packet it processed.

```
[0..3]  outer reliable header   [03][seq LE2][rt=0x08]
[4..]   inner ACK payload (size depends on form)
```

Inner forms:

| Inner size | Layout                              | Count |
|------------|-------------------------------------|-------|
| 2 bytes    | `[ack_seq LE16]`                    | 46/54 |
| 4 bytes    | `[ack_seq LE16][00 00]` zero pad    |  8/54 |

The 2-byte form's `ack_seq` value precisely tracks `seq - 1` of
the carrier reliable wrapper — i.e. the client emits one
ACK-08 sub-packet per server-reliable received, and the
ack_seq it reports is the seq of the just-received S→C
reliable. (In C→S retail data the carrier seq and the inner
ack_seq advance in lockstep because client emits an ack right
after each server-reliable.)

Verified samples (cross-pcap):

```
PLAZA_TO_PEPPER_CROSS_DISTRICT  carrier seq=0x0451  inner=50 04   (=0x0450)
RETAIL_CHARDEL_SUBWAY           carrier seq=0x0694  inner=93 06   (=0x0693)
RETAIL_LONG_PARTY_A             carrier seq=0x09d2  inner=d1 09   (=0x09d1)
RETAIL_LONG_PARTY_A             carrier seq=0x05dd  inner=dc 05   (=0x05dc)
CREATION_LEVELING_LONG          carrier seq=0x0001  inner=00 00   (initial)
```

The 4-byte form appears only at session-start (carrier seq=1)
in 2 captures (CREATION_LEVELING_LONG, FULL_PCAP_TRACE) — a
zero-padded early-handshake variant.

## Variants

- **2-byte ACK**: dominant — runtime reliable acks.
- **4-byte ACK**: only first ack at session start (8/54).
  Indistinguishable from the 2-byte form except for the trailing
  `00 00` pad. Likely a residual handshake-era padding.

## Observed contexts

Emitted any time the client receives a reliable S→C packet it
needs to acknowledge — typically after `0x03/0x07` multipart
chains complete or after specific reliable-flagged S→C
broadcasts. Top markers tie this to mid-gameplay events
(`OUTSIDE_AREAM5_GENREP_TELE`, etc.).

Most active in `RETAIL_VEHICLE_DRONE` (22 emissions) — high
activity correlates with frequent vehicle/drone state updates
the client is acking.

## Open questions

- Why the 4-byte form only at session start? Is the first ack
  encoded with an extra `00 00` for handshake-handshake padding,
  or is it a different sub-action implicitly (with implicit
  zero suffix)?
- Does the server validate the ack_seq matches its emitted
  reliable-seq, or just consume the ack as a flow-control
  signal?
- Multi-ack: do clients ever batch (`[seq1][seq2]…`) in one
  0x08 frame? No retail evidence — all samples are 1 ack.

## Server-side handler

`ReliableAckSubPacket.execute()` already wired to consume this
packet in retransmit-ring lookup mode. Path:
1. Read inner LE16 = ack_seq
2. Lookup in `pl.getUdpConnection().reliableRing().get(seq)` —
   if found, retransmit via PacketBuilderUDP1302
3. If not found, NO-OP (ack already-received case)

This was implemented in task #136 (P1 retransmit-ring) and
gated on `pl.isloggedin()` per
`harness_unreplicable_broadcasts.md` — production path
always-true post-handshake; harness fixture always-false to
suppress noise.

The 4-byte zero-padded form is consumed identically (only
bytes [0..1] are read; trailing zeros are ignored).
